vocabulary.definition: Any side of a triangle used to calculate area
vocabulary.visual: If the bottom of a triangle is 10 cm, then b = 10 cm in the formula A = ½ × b × h

vocabulary.definition: The perpendicular distance from the base to the opposite vertex
vocabulary.visual: A dashed vertical line from the top point straight down to the base at a 90° angle — like dropping a plumb line

vocabulary.definition: The amount of space inside a two-dimensional figure
vocabulary.visual: A triangle with b = 8 and h = 6 has area = ½ × 8 × 6 = 24 sq units — exactly half the rectangle around it

vocabulary.definition: Lines or segments that meet at a 90-degree angle
vocabulary.visual: The corner of a book or a door frame — the edges meet at exactly 90°, shown by a small square symbol

vocabulary.definition: A shape made of two or more basic shapes combined
vocabulary.visual: A house shape = a rectangle (the walls) + a triangle (the roof); total area = rectangle area + triangle area

vocabulary.definition: A mathematical rule written with symbols
vocabulary.visual: A = ½ × b × h means Area equals one-half times base times height; for b = 12 and h = 8, A = 48

launch.narrative: Your architecture firm is designing a park with triangular garden beds. The client wants to know exactly how much soil to order, which means calculating the area of each triangular section. The first garden has a base of 12 feet and a height of 8 feet.
launch.contextImage: Architectural blueprint with triangular garden sections marked in green
noticePrompts: What shape are the garden beds?
What measurements do we need to find the area?
How is the area of a triangle related to the area of a rectangle?
wonderPrompts: Why do we divide by 2 when finding the area of a triangle?
Does it matter which side we call the base?

explore.instructions: Plot the vertices of a triangle with base 12 and height 8 to visualize the garden. Place points at (0, 0), (12, 0), and (6, 8).
explore.label: Plot the three vertices of the triangular garden bed.

discourse.prompt: The triangle fits inside a 12 × 8 rectangle. How does the triangle's area compare to the rectangle's area?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The area of the triangle is ___ the rectangle because A = 1/2 × ___ × ___ = ___ square feet.

approaching.stem: What is the area of a triangle with base 10 cm and height 6 cm?
approaching.explanation: A = ½ × b × h = ½ × 10 × 6 = 30 square centimeters.

approaching.stem: A triangle has an area of 24 sq ft and a base of 8 ft. What is the height?
Why do we divide by 2 when finding the area of a triangle?
choices: A triangle is exactly half of a rectangle with the same base and height
The base is always twice the height
We always divide area formulas by 2
approaching.explanation: A triangle with base b and height h fits inside a rectangle with the same base and height. The triangle covers exactly half the rectangle, so A = ½ × b × h.

approaching.stem: What is the area of a triangle with base 14 ft and height 6 ft?

approaching.instructions: Use the formula A = ½ × b × h to find the area of each triangle.

onLevel.label: Match each shape to its area.

onLevel.instructions: Calculate the area of each triangle. Sort by whether the area is greater than 30 sq units or not.
A triangle has a base of 10 ft and an area of 35 sq ft. Place a point at the height. (Hint: 35 = ½ × 10 × h → 35 = 5h → h = 7)
onLevel.label: Find the missing height: b = 10, A = 35

extending.title: Find the Area Error
extending.correctWork: 14 × 9 = 126, then ½ × 126 = 63 sq m. The student added 14 + 9 = 23 instead of multiplying 14 × 9 = 126.
hints: Look at Step 4. What operation should happen between 14 and 9?
14 × 9 ≠ 23. The student added instead of multiplied.

extending.prompt: A rectangle is 12 ft by 8 ft. A diagonal line cuts it into two triangles. What is the area of each triangle? Explain how the triangle area formula relates to the rectangle area formula.
extending.sentenceFrame: The rectangle's area is ___ because ___. Each triangle's area is ___ because a diagonal splits a rectangle into ___ equal triangles, so A = ½ × ___ × ___ = ___.

extending.instructions: A triangular garden has base 16 ft and height 10 ft. The bar model shows the rectangle area (whole) and the triangle area (part). Fill in the values.
extending.label: Rectangle 16×10 = 160 sq ft split into two equal triangles of 80 sq ft each

connect.scenario: A homeowner wants to paint one triangular wall of an A-frame cabin. The wall has a base of 20 feet and a height of 14 feet. One can of paint covers 50 square feet.
connect.promptQuestion: How many cans of paint does the homeowner need?
connect.prompt: The wall's area is ___ sq ft because A = 1/2 × ___ × ___. She needs ___ cans because ___ ÷ 50 = ___.

exitTicket.stem: A triangle has a base of 11 inches and a height of 8 inches. What is its area?
